obituaries.name: Maria Celia Perez Chavez
obituaries.summary: Maria Celia Perez Chavez December 4, 1950 — March 3, 2026 Maria Celia Perez Chavez, age 75, of West, passed away Tuesday morning, March 3, 2026 at Ascension Providence Hospital in Waco. A rosary will be recited 5:00 p.m. Monday at Aderhold Funeral Home, followed by visitation until 7:00 p.m. Mass of Christian Burial will be celebrated 11:00 a.m. Tuesday, March 10, 2026 at St. Mary's Catholic Church of the Assumption, with Rev. Timothy Vaverek as celebrant. Burial will follow at St. Mary's Cemetery. Celia was born on December 4, 1950, in Chalchihuites, Mexico, to Florencio and Florencia (Serrato) Chavez Lopez. She began her early education at Vasdespino Catholic School and carried her learned

obituaries.name: Betty Stasny Belcher
obituaries.summary: Betty Sybil Stasny Belcher was born on April 29, 1924 to Frank J. Stasny and Rosie K. Sirny Stasny in Taylor, Texas. On April 2, 1948 she married RW (Buddy) Belcher in Bryan, Texas. They were married 51 years when her husband passed away on July 4, 1999. She was a homemaker and she enjoyed making her home nice and taking care of her plants. She also enjoyed camping, fishing, and being with family. She was preceded in death by her husband, RW (Buddy) Belcher; parents, Frank J. Stasny and Rosie K. Sirny Stasny; sister, Julia R. Barrington; niece, Doris Jean Driska; and brother-in-law, Willie B. Driska. She is survived by her sister, Ella Marie Driska; nieces, Rosanne Glinski, Theresa Driska, J

obituaries.name: Stefanie Reed
obituaries.summary: Stefanie Reed November 5, 1971 — March 1, 2026 Stefanie Dionne (Walts) Reed, age 54, of West, passed away early Sunday morning, March 1, 2026 at Baylor Scott & White Hillcrest Hospital in Waco. The family will receive visitors from 5:00 to 6:30 p.m. today Monday, March 2, 2026 at the West Church of Christ. Memorial Services will be held at a later date. Stefanie was born on November 5, 1971, in Waco, the cherished daughter of Michael Walts and Diane (Neel) Walts Parma. A proud 1990 graduate of West High School, Stefanie furthered her education at McLennan Community College where she was recognized on the Dean's List. Throughout her career, she dedicated herself to serving others, working fir

obituaries.name: Sharon Virginia Holmes
obituaries.summary: Sharon Virginia Holmes August 19, 1947 — March 1, 2026 Kosse Sharon Virginia (DuBose) Holmes, 78, passed away on Sunday, March 1, 2026. Funeral services will be 10:00 a.m., Saturday, March 7, at Pecan Grove Funeral Home, with Pastor Robert Nelson officiating. Burial will follow at Valley Mills Cemetery. The family will receive visitors from 5 – 7:00 p.m., Friday, March 6, at the funeral home. Sharon was born in DeRidder, Louisiana, to Aubrey and Pinnice (Whiddon) DuBose. She married Curtis Calvin Holmes on September 7, 1963. She faithfully supported her husband in his calling to serve the Lord, standing beside him with strength and unwavering devotion. She was a prayer warrior that spent hou

obituaries.name: Donald Jon Cann
obituaries.summary: Donald Jon Cann October 31, 1938 — March 1, 2026 Sumter, South Carolina Listen to Obituary The Grand Adventure Begins Lt. Col. Donald J. Cann began his life appropriately on Halloween in 1938 — a day well-suited for a man who would spend 87 years delighting in surprise, mischief, and good stories. On March 1, 2026, he took his final journey while on a road trip — exactly where he would have wanted to be — somewhere between a new destination and a promising local restaurant. He did not so much pass away as set off ahead of us, itinerary in hand. The Boy Who Explored Everything Long before he flew missions or handed out travel spreadsheets, Don was roaming fishing banks, hiking wooded trails,

obituaries.name: James Howard Pitts
obituaries.summary: James Howard Pitts March 12, 1936 — February 28, 2026 Bruceville, Texas Listen to Obituary James Howard "Killer" Pitts of Lorena, Texas passed away peacefully, surrounded by family on Saturday, February 28, 2026. He was born in Dawson, Texas on March 12, 1936. He graduated from Dawson High School and excelled in football, track and baseball. He was an avid golfer and lifelong Baylor Bear football fan. James retired from LeHigh cement company where he was a supervisor for more than thirty years. He was preceded in death by his parents, Howard and Iona Pitts and a sister, Beverly Pitts. He is survived by his wife, Nancy, of 66 years, and his three children, James "Jay" Pitts of Missoula, Monta

obituaries.name: Amy Rene Morris
obituaries.summary: Amy Rene Morris February 13, 1978 - February 28, 2026 Amy Morris February 13, 1978 ~ February 28, 2026 Amy got her wings on February 28, 2026, at 11:22 P.M. She enjoyed fishing, round-track racing, and being around friends. She was a good person who would help anyone. She would put others before her at times. She was preceded in death by her grandparents, James A. and Betty Brown, and her aunt, Cathy Brown. obituaries.summary: Linda Fae Hineline July 15, 1944 — February 27, 2026 Waco, Texas Listen to Obituary Linda Fae Hineline, age 81, of Waco, Texas, passed away peacefully on February 27, 2026, in Waco. A celebration of her life will be held at 11:00 a.m. on May 22, 2026, at Central Texas State Veterans Cemetery in Killeen, Texas. Linda was born on July 15, 1944, in Waterloo, Iowa, to James William Norfolk and Helen Fae Thompson Norfolk. From an early age, she found joy in art and reading, and she cherished time spent with her family and beloved pets. Those early passions for creativity and connection remained constant throughout her life. A woman of remarkable strength and versatility, Linda lived a life define

obituaries.name: Daniel Shown
obituaries.summary: Daniel Shown August 23, 1959 — February 27, 2026 Daniel Robert Shown was born on August 23, 1959, to John Ben Shown and Earnestine Yarbrough. Raised in Waco, Texas, Daniel grew up with strong values of faith, family, and service—principles that would guide him throughout his life. As a young man, Daniel was a standout athlete for the La Vega Pirates in Waco. His talent and determination on the gridiron earned him a football scholarship to Cisco Junior College, where he played linebacker for two years. While at Cisco, Daniel obtained an associate degree in Criminal Justice, laying the foundation for the career he had dreamed of since high school. In June 1980, Daniel was accepted into the Tex

obituaries.name: Carol Ann Jortner
obituaries.summary: Carol Ann Jortner November 17, 1939 — February 27, 2026 Waco Carol Ann Jortner, 86, of Waco, passed away Friday, February 27, 2026, in Waco. A graveside service will be held at 1:00 p.m. Wednesday, March 4, at Rodef Sholom Cemetery in Waco. Carol was born November 17, 1939, in New York City to John Effinger and Winifred Lee Effinger. She later graduated with a B.A. from Hillins College. She was an educator during her working career. She was preceded in death by her parents and her husband, Bernard Jortner. Carol is survived by her daughter, Susan Deely of Georgetown; her sons, David Jortner of Hewitt and Adam Jortner of Wolfville, Nova Scotia; and seven grandchildren. The family invites you

obituaries.name: Barbara Helen Caudle
obituaries.summary: Barbara Helen Caudle July 17, 1938 — February 27, 2026 Waco, Texas formerly of Abilene, Texas Listen to Obituary Barbara Helen Caudle, 87, formerly of Abilene, passed away peacefully at Living Springs in Waco, TX. Graveside services will be held Wednesday at 2:00 pm at Cottonwood Cemetery. Born July 17, 1938, in Throckmorton to Horace and Essie Peacock, Helen was a woman of professional excellence and deep family devotion. A skilled administrative assistant, she served at Catalina Foothills Schools, General Dynamics/Lockheed, and Hendrick Hospital. She was preceded in death by her parents, sisters Jean and Joan, and husbands Robert Smith and Dale Caudle. She is survived by her children, Debb

obituaries.name: James W. Harrison
obituaries.summary: James W. Harrison August 23, 1925 — February 26, 2026 Waco James W. Harrison, 100, of Waco, passed away Thursday, February 26, 2026. A private family burial will be held at White Rock Cemetery in Ross. In his own words as recently expressed: "I have had a wonderful life. I was married for 62 years to a beautiful, incredible woman. We were blessed with 3 great and loving children; 5 grandchildren; and 7 great grandchildren. I had a fulfilling career for which I was able to travel all over the world, as project manager for Dow Chemical. I have been greatly blessed in my long life" Thanks for everything, dad. May you rest in a well-earned peace in the hands of a loving God. Linda, James, and Da

obituaries.summary: Pat Wyatt February 1, 1955 — February 25, 2026 Patricia Ann (Davis) Wyatt, beloved mother, grandmother, and compassionate friend, age 71, of Axtell , passed away Wednesday evening, February 25, 2026 in Axtell. A Funeral Service will be held 10:00 a.m. Friday, March 6, 2026 at Billington Church in Axtell, with Bro. Kerry Groce officiating. Burial will follow at nearby Billington Cemetery. Pat was born February 1, 1955 in Waco, the daughter of Lewis and Wanda (Brady) Davis. She was a graduate of R.L. Turner High School in Carrollton , Class of 1973, and furthered her education with college courses at McLennan Community College. On February 28, 1976, Pat married the love of her life, Floyd Wyat

obituaries.name: Jerry Lynn Ballard
obituaries.summary: Jerry Lynn Ballard August 23, 1943 — February 25, 2026 Waco Jerry Lynn Ballard, age 82, passed away Wednesday, February 25, 2026, at Providence Hospital in Waco. The funeral service will be held at 12:30 p.m. Tuesday, March 3, 2026, at Wilkirson-Hatch-Bailey Funeral Home, with Minister Corbin Smith officiating. Burial will follow at Waco Memorial Park. Visitation will begin one hour prior to the service at Wilkirson-Hatch-Bailey Funeral Home. Born in Mexia, Texas, on August 23, 1943, Jerry grew up in Mexia and attended Mexia High School. obituaries.name: Bettie Jean Reaves
obituaries.summary: Bettie Jean Reaves February 16, 1931 — February 24, 2026 Hewitt Bettie Jean Reaves, 95, of Waco, Texas, passed away peacefully on February 24, 2026, just days after celebrating her birthday. Bettie was born to Sherman and Edith (Mackie) Mills in Waco, Texas. She grew up in South Waco near the 15th Street area and later moved to Rosenthal, Texas, where she attended school before graduating from Waco High School. Bettie was a long-time member of Hewitt First Baptist Church. In 1950, Bettie married Arno Frosch, and together they welcomed two children, David and Sherrie. obituaries.name: Patricia Ann Schwarz
obituaries.summary: Patricia Ann Schwarz February 13, 1948 — February 22, 2026 Waco, Texas Listen to Obituary Patricia Ann McGee Schwarz passed away peacefully at home in Waco, Texas on Sunday, February 22, 2026. She was born on February 13, 1948, in Ponca City, Oklahoma, to Robert Vernon McGee and Margie Ruth Holloway McGee. She graduated from Sulphur High School in Louisiana, and completed a Bachelor of Education from McNeese State University in Baton Rouge, Louisiana. She taught school for 28 years including a long tenure at Kendrick Elementary School in Waco. She is survived by her husband of forty-three years, Steven Wayne Schwarz of Waco; son Steven Robert Schwarz and wife Kate and grandchildren Riley and

obituaries.name: Joseph Allen Salazar
obituaries.summary: Joseph Allen Salazar, born on September 4, 1980, in Waco, Texas to Laura and Mark Salazar, passed away peacefully on February 22, 2026, at the age of 45. While our hearts are heavy with loss, we find comfort in knowing he is now at peace in heaven and embraced in the loving presence of the Lord. Growing up, Joseph was always the “all-time” quarterback for his two brothers, a title he carried proudly. He loved taking trips to visit Aunt Jana’s house to spend time with his cousins Michael and Robert, and he looked forward to visits at Aunt Annie’s where everyone would gather and play board games into the late hours of the night. Those moments were filled with laughter and friendly competition

obituaries.name: Ann Hough Obituary February 22, 2026 - Grace Gardens Funeral Home & Crematorium
obituaries.summary: Ann is survived by her husband of 28 years, Michael Hough, 4 daughters Jennifer Washburn, Shawna Orlandos, Elise Baker, Julie Hough and 3 grandchildren as well as her brother Bill Swift. Ann and Mike are residents of Woodway Texas having retired in early 2023 moving here from California where Ann was an Auditor. Ann was very loved and known for her smile and servant’s heart. Ann and Mike are members of Crossroads Fellowship Church. They had adopted Baylor University and could often be found at various sporting events. Ann was active in various ministries that focus on feeding the homeless and ministering to female inmates. For the last two years she served as treasurer for MCRW stepping down

obituaries.summary: Juanita Melendez May 14, 1937 — February 21, 2026 Waco Juanita Melendez, age 88, of Waco, Texas, entered into eternal rest at her home on February 21, 2026, and went to be with her Lord and Savior, Jesus Christ. A viewing will be held at Wilkirson-Hatch-Bailey Funeral Home in Waco on Friday, February 27, 2026, from 5:00 p.m. until 7:00 p.m. A celebration of Janie’s life will be held the next morning at 10:00 a.m. on Saturday, February 28, in the chapel at Wilkirson-Hatch-Bailey. Burial will follow at Oakwood Cemetery, with Pastor Pete Arvizu presiding. obituaries.name: Helen Jo Hurst
obituaries.summary: Helen Jo Hurst June 24, 1943 — February 21, 2026 Helen Jo Hurst, 82, of Axtell, Texas, passed away peacefully on February 21, 2026 at her residence. Funeral Service will be held 10:00 a.m. Friday, February 27, 2026 at Aderhold Funeral Home Chapel in West, with Eddie Scheler officiating. Graveside Service will be held 11:30 a.m. Friday at Axtell Cemetery. The family will receive visitors from 5:00 to 7:00 p.m. Thursday at the funeral home. Born in West, Texas, on June 24, 1943, Helen dedicated much of her life to caring for others as a Licensed Vocational Nurse (LVN) at many offices including Groesbeck State Detention Center, Meridian Hospital and Clinic, and Hill Regional Hospital. She spent

obituaries.name: Evencio Marin Munguia
obituaries.summary: Evencio Marín Munguía nació en Villa de Allende, Estado de México, el 3 de mayo de 1949. Pasó toda su infancia en una comunidad con sus padres y hermanos. Completó su formación docente y posteriormente estudió en el Seminario Diocesano de Toluca, pero no concluyó sus estudios. Se casó con Juanita González García por lo civil y por la iglesia. Tuvieron cuatro hijos: Jesús, Janeth, Diana y Nancy Marín. Dedicó su vida a trabajar en su propia barbería en Villa de Allende durante cincuenta años, dejando su legado a sus hijos, Jesús y Janeth. obituaries.name: Connie Jean Gordon
obituaries.summary: Connie Jean Gordon March 1, 1953 — February 21, 2026 Connie Jean Gordon, 72, of Ross, Texas, passed away Saturday, February 21, 2026. Memorial service will be held at 3:00 p.m. on Thursday, March 5, 2026, at Bellmead Funeral Home Chapel. Connie was preceded in death by her husband of 50 years, James Michael Gordon of Ross; her mother, Glenda McGee; and her father, Walter McGee of Bellmead. She is survived by her two sons, Michael Wayne Gordon of Ross and Christopher James Gordon of Bellmead. She also leaves behind five grandchildren: Kinsee Carlton and her husband, Tylan Carlton; Courtney Hannemann and her fiancé, Johnathon Flores; Kimbree Gordon; Lindsey Gordon; and Gracie Gordon. obituaries.name: Traci Alisha Fischer Obituary February 20, 2026 - Grace Gardens Funeral Home & Crematorium
obituaries.summary: Traci Fischer (Hill) went home to be with her Lord and Savior Jesus on 20 Feb 2026 after being ill for several years. She was born on 26 Sep 1970 in Greenwood, MS to Charles and Terry Hill. She excelled in sports and survived a devastating softball injury in 1982. She's a proud alumnus of Mississippi State University, was a member of the Phi Mu Sorority and graduated with a Bachelor's Degree in Communications. She met her husband Paul through Harmony met each other for the first time and watched the Mississippi Riverkings hockey team and eventually would attend many more games together. Traci is survived by her mother Terry Hill, preceded in death by her father Charles Hill, her older sister

obituaries.name: Rosemarie Adames
obituaries.summary: Rosemarie Adames June 13, 1954 — February 20, 2026 Waco Rosemarie Adames, 71, of Waco, passed away Tuesday, February 20, 2026, at her home. Visitation services will be held on Saturday, February 28, from 3:30 p.m. to 5:30 p.m. at Wilkirson-Hatch-Bailey Funeral Home, 6101 Bosque Blvd., Waco, TX 76710. Please join the family for a light reception immediately following the service at Sacred Heart Church Hall, 2621 Bagby Ave., Waco, TX. Rosemarie was born in Waco, Texas, at Hillcrest Hospital. She grew up in Waco, attended Waco High School, and earned her nursing degree. She married Israel Adames on May 13, 1980. After many dedicated years, she retired from Allergan. obituaries.name: Linda Kay Berry
obituaries.summary: Linda Kay Berry July 17, 1942 — February 20, 2026 Waco Linda “Kay” Berry, 83, of Waco, passed away peacefully Friday, February 20, 2026, in Waco. A celebration of her life will be held at 12:30 p.m. Saturday, February 28, at Wilkirson-Hatch-Bailey Funeral Home with Pastor Roy Marshall officiating. Kay was born to Norman Russell and Mary Elliston Berry on July 17, 1942, in Seagraves, TX. After the family moved to Abbott, Texas, she graduated as the Salutatorian of Abbott High School and began her university studies at Baylor University. After graduating with her bachelor’s degree, she continued her education and earned a master’s degree in social work from Louisiana Tech University. She began

obituaries.name: Carol McGuire
obituaries.summary: Carol McGuire, 87, of Waco, TX passed away on February 20, just two days shy of her eighty-eighth birthday. The funeral service will be held at 2:00 p.m., on Wednesday, March 4, at OakCrest Funeral Home with visitation at 1:00 p.m. Private burial will be at a later date. Born Carol Sue Bertram on February 22, 1938 in Lamesa, Texas to Thomas and Montez Bertram, she was raised in Hobbs, NM, the oldest of four siblings. Carol attended college at McMurry University in Abilene, singing in the college choir, the Chanters.
